UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile and application-wide settings within Demokratia Portugal, excluding authentication credentials.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

username.description: A unique username or identifier for the user within the application.

email.description: The user's email address, primarily for communication. Authentication is managed externally.

preferredLanguage.description: The user's preferred language for the application interface (e.g., 'pt' for Portuguese, 'en' for English).

themePreference.description: The user's preferred UI theme, such as 'light', 'dark', or 'system'.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was last updated.

SimulationScenario.title: SimulationScenario
SimulationScenario.description: Defines a hypothetical political and economic scenario, including user-specified variables, for use in the economic simulator.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SimulationScenario entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile that created this simulation scenario. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N SimulationScenario)

name.description: A human-readable name for the simulation scenario, provided by the user.

description.description: A detailed explanation or notes about the simulation scenario, including its objectives or assumptions.

parameters.description: JSON string representing the hypothetical political and economic variables and their specific values defined for this scenario. This allows for flexible and dynamic scenario inputs.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the simulation scenario was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the simulation scenario was last updated.

SimulationRun.title: SimulationRun
SimulationRun.description: Stores the results of a specific execution of a simulation scenario at a given point in time.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SimulationRun entity.

scenarioId.description: Reference to the SimulationScenario that was executed in this run. (Relationship: SimulationScenario 1:N SimulationRun)

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who initiated this simulation run. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N SimulationRun)

runAt.description: Timestamp indicating when this simulation run was executed.

inputParametersSnapshot.description: JSON string representing a snapshot of the scenario's parameters at the exact time of this simulation run, ensuring reproducibility.

outputResults.description: JSON string containing the detailed simulated economic impact data and analytical results generated by the model for this run.

dataSourcesUsed.description: An array of strings, each identifying a specific external data source (e.g., 'WorldBank', 'Eurostat', 'INE Portugal') used to inform this simulation run.

notes.description: Optional user-provided notes or observations about this specific simulation run and its results.

SavedDataExploration.title: SavedDataExploration
SavedDataExploration.description: Allows users to save and revisit specific configurations or views within the Public Data Explorer.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SavedDataExploration entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who saved this data exploration configuration. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N SavedDataExploration)

name.description: A user-friendly name for the saved data exploration view.

description.description: A detailed description of what this saved exploration represents, including the types of data, filters, or insights it provides.

configuration.description: JSON string defining the specific filters, selected external data sources, visualization settings, and other parameters that constitute this saved data exploration view.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when this data exploration configuration was saved.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when this data exploration configuration was last updated.

lastAccessedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time this saved data exploration was accessed or viewed by the user.

PublicData.title: PublicData
PublicData.description: Represents a public data set, such as economic indicators.

label.description: The human-readable name of the data set.

description.description: A brief description of the data set.

unit.description: The unit of measurement for the data values.

data.description: An array of data points for the data set.

StatisticalData.title: StatisticalData
StatisticalData.description: Represents a more complex or structured public data set for the Data Explorer.

title.description: The human-readable title of the data set.

description.description: A brief description of the data set and its context.

source.description: The official source of the data (e.g., 'INE, Censos 2021').

lastUpdated.description: Timestamp indicating when the data was last updated.

dataType.description: Hint for the UI on how to best display the data (e.g., 'table', 'barchart').

data.description: A JSON string containing the structured data, which can be an array of objects or any other format.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles and application settings. Access is restricted to the authenticated user matching the {userId}. The 'id' field in the document matches the {userId} in the path for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, corresponding to Firebase Authentication's UID.

definition.description: Stores user-defined hypothetical political and economic simulation scenarios. Each scenario is owned by a specific user. Includes denormalized 'userId' field for authorization independence, ensuring it matches the parent path's {userId}.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns this simulation scenario.

params.name: scenarioId
params.description: The unique ID for a specific simulation scenario.

definition.description: Stores the results of specific executions of a simulation scenario. These runs are nested under their respective scenarios and users. Includes denormalized 'userId' and 'scenarioId' fields for authorization independence, ensuring they match the respective path segments.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who initiated this simulation run.

params.name: scenarioId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent simulation scenario.

params.name: runId
params.description: The unique ID for a specific simulation run.

definition.description: Allows users to save and revisit specific configurations of the Public Data Explorer. Each saved exploration is owned by a specific user. Includes denormalized 'userId' field for authorization independence, ensuring it matches the parent path's {userId}.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who saved this data exploration configuration.

params.name: explorationId
params.description: The unique ID for a specific saved data exploration.

definition.description: Stores public data sets that are accessible to all users.

params.name: dataSetId
params.description: The unique ID for a specific public data set (e.g., 'gdp', 'unemployment').

definition.description: Stores complex public data sets for the Data Explorer, accessible to all users.

params.name: dataId
params.description: The unique ID for a specific statistical data set.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for Demokratia Portugal is designed with a strong emphasis on authorization independence, structural segregation, and predictable access modeling, aligning with the core design principles to ensure security, scalability, and debuggability. All user-specific data is nested under a top-level `/users/{userId}` collection. This path-based ownership ensures that all documents belonging to a specific user are logically grouped and easily secured. A new top-level collection `publicData` is introduced for information that should be accessible by all users, following the principle of structural segregation for public vs. private data.

**Authorization Independence:** This is achieved by explicitly denormalizing ownership IDs into the documents themselves. For instance, `SimulationScenario` documents are stored under `/users/{userId}/simulationScenarios/{scenarioId}`. Crucially, each `SimulationScenario` document will *also* contain a `userId` field that matches the `{userId}` in its path. Similarly, `SimulationRun` documents, nested under `/users/{userId}/simulationScenarios/{scenarioId}/simulationRuns/{runId}`, will contain both `userId` and `scenarioId` fields that match their respective path segments. This strategy eliminates the need for `get()` calls in Firestore Security Rules to check parent document ownership or attributes, thus enabling atomic operations and simplifying rule logic. Rules can directly check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` (and `request.path[3] == resource.data.scenarioId` for `SimulationRun`) which is highly performant and secure.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** The structural segregation inherently supports secure `list` operations. By organizing user-owned data under `/users/{userId}/...`, users can only perform `list` queries within their own `/users/{request.auth.uid}/...` subcollections. This prevents accidental exposure of other users' data during collection-level queries, as the security rules will enforce that `request.auth.uid` matches the `userId` in the path segment for any read operation. The data structure itself acts as a filter, allowing users to query only data explicitly owned by them, making security rules simple and efficient for both `get` and `list` operations. There are no global collections mixing different ownership types, ensuring a homogeneous security posture per collection.

**Structural Segregation & Access Modeling:** All collections like `simulationScenarios`, `simulationRuns`, and `savedDataExplorations` within a user's path are homogenous in their security requirements – they are strictly owned by that user. The `publicData` collection is separate and has its own distinct, public-read security model. The addition of the `statisticalData` collection follows this same principle, providing a new top-level, public-read collection for more complex data sets.
